authoritative_sources.title: P 120 Public Buildings Service Cost and Schedule Management Policy
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA is an authoritative public construction owner. P 120 defines lifecycle cost and schedule governance, estimate classes, management deliverables, and control expectations directly relevant to construction cost-management planning.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Cost-governance framing and baseline recommendation boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Develop and Manage Costs for RWA Projects
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: GSA publishes official public cost-management guidance that distinguishes estimate types, estimate maturity, and control expectations across project delivery. It is authoritative for forecast and estimate handling inside this lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Project Requirements: Project Sign-Off and Completion
authoritative_sources.publisher: New York City Department of Buildings
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: NYC DOB is an authority having jurisdiction. Its sign-off guidance defines inspection, objection-resolution, as-built, and completion prerequisites that materially affect final-cost closure and closeout posture.

authoritative_sources.title: 29 CFR 1926.20 - General safety and health provisions
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: OSHA is the primary U.S. construction-safety regulator. This standard defines accident-prevention and inspection duties that cost-management recommendations cannot override when safety conditions materially change the answer.
used_for: refusal when unsafe conditions change the answer

authoritative_sources.title: Cost Estimating
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: WBDG is a recognized public building guidance platform used by federal and industry practitioners. Its cost-estimating guidance defines estimate purpose, methods, and documentation expectations that keep this lane specific to construction cost management.
used_for: documentation expectations for estimate and forecast packages

authoritative_sources.title: EP 415-1-260 Area/Resident Engineer Management Guide
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: USACE is an authoritative public construction owner. This guide gives explicit change-pricing, contingency, and construction-administration guidance that directly informs exception handling and approval boundaries in the lane.

authoritative_sources.title: Construction Manual, Section 5-1: Project Records and Reports
authoritative_sources.source_class: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: Caltrans publishes an official owner-side construction manual defining daily reports, project records, quantity and change documentation, and estimate-supporting field evidence. It is authoritative for traceability and record discipline in cost-management workflows.

workflow_stages.name: Demand intake and cost-control normalization
workflow_stages.description: Classify the request, confirm project, site, permit, cost-code, estimate, and tenant approval context, and decide whether cost-management work can start safely.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Constraint and capacity assessment
workflow_stages.description: Assess review capacity, committed-cost state, subcontractor coverage, permit and site-readiness constraints, inspection dependencies, and closeout posture before recommending timing or allocation.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Schedule, priority, and allocation recommendation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare recommendation-only sequencing, deferral, forecast, and tradeoff outputs while preserving explicit assumptions and avoiding final approval posture.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards

workflow_stages.name: Permit, inspection, safety, and closeout gating
workflow_stages.description: Stop recommendations when permit, inspection, safety, objection-resolution, or closeout conditions materially change whether work, payment posture, or final-cost closure can proceed.
source_classes: Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

workflow_stages.name: Forecast refresh and exception escalation
workflow_stages.description: Package changed conditions, policy conflicts, approval-needed tradeoffs, and authority-bound exceptions instead of synthesizing unsupported cost certainty.
source_classes: Scheduling, subcontractor, or work-order operating standards
Public permitting, inspection, or field-service guidance

artifacts: work orders, project identifiers, and cost codes
estimate packages, basis-of-estimate records, and forecast revisions
schedule of values, committed-cost ledgers, and cost reports
potential, pending, and approved change orders
subcontractor quotes, buyout logs, and invoice or pay-application support
permits, approved plans, inspection records, and correction notices
site logs, daily reports, photos, and safety observations
punch lists, sign-off records, turnover documentation, and closeout packages

allowed: Assess whether cost-management records and public-baseline prerequisites support a bounded recommendation on sequencing, allocation, forecast, or blocker posture.
Identify capacity, permit, inspection, site, subcontractor, and closeout blockers for transparent cost-control planning.
Prepare evidence-backed recommendation packages, constraint logs, approval-needed summaries, and escalation notes without claiming final approval authority.
escalate: Permit, inspection, safety, or closeout conditions that materially change whether cost assumptions remain valid
Conflicting tenant policy and public baseline on cost closeout, payment posture, or approval sequencing
Requests that move into project controls, permitting, superintendent, licensed trade, inspection, or safety ownership
refuse: Final budget approval, contingency draw approval, payment certification, permit approval, code interpretation, or licensed trade signoff
Any request to bypass site-safety, inspection, unresolved objections, or closeout evidence requirements
Deterministic cost or payment recommendations when required tenant facts or authoritative records are missing

domain_failure_modes: recommending cost or payment posture from stale estimate basis, unresolved permit assumptions, or missing approved-plan context
treating provisional field completion as final-cost closeout while inspections, objections, or turnover records are still open
allowing schedule or budget pressure to override new safety observations or failed inspection outcomes
collapsing construction cost management into generic prioritization without documenting cost-code, subcontractor, site-readiness, or closeout dependencies
acting as code interpreter, licensed trade authority, superintendent, or final commercial approver instead of escalating

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Request that implies licensed trade or code-interpretation authority
Demand exceeds capacity or approved service levels
Conflicting priorities with no approved tie-break rule
Stakeholder asks for final approval the lane cannot grant

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant contract language, cost-code governance, approval thresholds, and payment-certification steps vary materially across owners, contractors, and industrial facilities.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The public baseline defines safe workflow boundaries, but runtime must retrieve tenant contract rules, authority matrices, and system-of-record precedence before asserting project-specific commercial posture.

notes: The research baseline intentionally covers workflow, evidence, terminology, and failure handling rather than generic finance or accounting only.
No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated spec-pack release because the remaining variation is explicitly routed to tenant-supplied runtime facts.
